steps.label: cause
steps.text: Grape spirit (neutral spirit high in alcohol) is added to a partially fermented grape must or wine

steps.label: mechanism
steps.text: The addition raises the alcohol content to a level (typically 15–18% ABV) at which yeast stops; yeast can no longer survive, fermentation halts and unfermented sugar remains in solution

steps.label: effect
steps.text: The finished wine retains residual sugar from the unfermented must, producing a sweet or medium-sweet style

steps.label: exam_formulation
steps.text: Fortification raises alcohol to a level at which yeast stops fermenting; fermentation ceases, retaining residual sugar in the finished wine.

sat_relevance: Use when assessing sweetness in fortified wines. Residual sugar in Port or sweet Sherry results directly from the timing of fortification — earlier addition means sweeter result.
